content:
Looking at the diagram, I need to identify the `types` package or equivalent grouping. The diagram uses namespaces like `Core_Layer`, `Extensions_Layer`, `Cpp_Layer`, etc. — there is no namespace explicitly called `types`.

The closest match would be the `Extensions_Layer` namespace, which contains the core type definitions (ArchJSON, Entity, Member, Relation, etc.) and the `Core_Layer` which also has domain types. However, neither is labeled `types`.

Examining all the relations shown in the diagrams, I need to find relations where both source and target nodes are defined in the same namespace. Looking specifically at `Extensions_Layer` (which contains the type/data structures):

Within `Extensions_Layer`, the classes defined are: `GoAtlasExtension`, `GoAtlasLayers`, `GoAtlasMetadata`, `PackageGraph`, `PackageCycle`, `PackageNode`, `PackageStats`, `PackageDependency`, `CapabilityGraph`, `CapabilityNode`, `CapabilityRelation`, `ConcreteUsageRisk`, `GoroutineLifecycleSummary`, `GoroutineTopology`, `GoroutineNode`, `SpawnRelation`, `ChannelInfo`, `ChannelEdge`, `FlowGraph`, `EntryPoint`, `CallChain`, `CallEdge`, `ArchJSONExtensions`, `TestPatternConfig`, `DetectedTestPatterns`, `TestAnalysis`, `TestFileInfo`, `CoverageLink`, `TestIssue`, `TestMetrics`, `PackageCoverage`, `TsAnalysis`, `TsModuleGraph`, `TsModuleNode`, `TsModuleDependency`, `TsModuleCycle`.

No explicit relations (arrows) are drawn between these classes within the `Extensions_Layer` namespace in the diagram.

There is no namespace named `types` in the provided diagram.
